_comment: High-precision SITE-PLAN title + drawing-marker vocabulary (2026-07-17; curated 2026-07-20 after the 2nd review; marker set narrowed 2026-07-20 after the 3rd review). Used by helpers/plan_signal.py to RESCUE a designed site-plan page the pixel classifier mis-reads as photo/map, and ONLY in that role. Store native orthography (case/accents); plan_signal folds both the lexicon and the page text via normalize._norm_city (accent/case-insensitive) plus apostrophe stripping. PRECISION RULES (a WRONG image in the trace-less Site Plan slot is worse than a miss, so keep this tight): (1) plan_titles are LAYOUT titles only - a to-scale SITE-LAYOUT drawing. DELIBERATELY EXCLUDES: amenity words (dock/yard/parking/clear height) = spec-sheet vocabulary; LOCATION / overview titles (Standortplan, Ubersichtsplan, plan de situation, plano de situacion, plan de incadrare, situatietekening) = a different artefact (a locator map, not the layout); and low-precision / false-friend titles (estate plan, plattegrond, bare planimetria = interior floor plan, plan du site = FR sitemap). Do NOT re-add these 'helpfully'. (2) drawing_markers here are the ENGLISH drawing-sheet PHRASES a to-scale drawing carries and an aerial/location map does not ('drawing no', 'sheet no', 'not to scale', ...); together with a to-scale scale RATIO they GATE the kind=='map' title-rescue (a titled 'map' page binds ONLY with a marker). NOTE: the SCALE-RATIO marker is NOT a token here - it is computed in plan_signal._scale_ratio_marker, which requires (a) a MULTILINGUAL scale-CUE word (scale/Maßstab/échelle/escala/schaal/skala/...) immediately before the ratio and (b) a SMALL, site-plan denominator (50..2500). Do NOT add 'scale 1:' or bare ratio tokens back: 'scale 1:' is magnitude-blind (it matched a 1:25000 LOCATOR scale bar exactly as a 1:500 plan = the 3rd-review wrong-bind) and bare '1:2500' matched inside '1:25000' via the isalpha() word boundary; and a BARE '1:N' with no cue matches a unit-schedule enumeration colon ('Unit 1: 500 sq m'), a clock/drive time ('1:50') or a dilution ('1:250') = the 4th-review wrong-bind. The cue list is multilingual, so the ratio path stays LANGUAGE-INDEPENDENT (no English word needed). Extend per market, keeping both rules.
